title: Novae Linguae Commons Bundle manifest (nlb/1)
description: The `manifest.json` member of a `.nlb` ("Nova Lingua Bundle") — a gzipped tar carrying `manifest.json`, `records.jsonl` (one content-addressed record per line, sorted by hash), and optionally `blobs/<sha256>` members: the blobs the bundled records reference (a by-address example value `examples[].result_blob`, a weights manifest file), carried so a restored record is CHECKABLE — not merely resolvable — on a node that never saw the origin. Blob members are self-verifying by NAME: the sha256 is recomputed on both write and read, and a mismatch refuses the bundle. A bundle is a portable, self-verifying archive for out-of-band redistribution (seed / disaster-recovery) and a release artifact any project can publish. The producer is UNTRUSTED: a node re-verifies every record by hash (and message signatures) on ingest, so the manifest is advisory metadata, never an admission gate (principle 7). `bundle_digest` is a cheap whole-payload integrity pre-check, not the security boundary. The optional `producer`/`signature` are advisory provenance ("this bundle came from X"), signed by the same canonical-serialization rules as a Nova Locutio message over the manifest with the `signature` field removed. Bundles are deterministic: the same content always produces identical bytes (records sorted by hash, blobs sorted by sha256, manifest keys sorted, fixed tar/gzip mtime); a blobless bundle is byte-identical to the pre-blob-carriage format. See spec/resilience.md and tooling/commons-node/commons/bundle.py.

format_version.description: The bundle format identifier. Bumped only on a breaking change to the archive layout or manifest shape.

schema_versions.description: The distinct record `schema_version` values present, sorted — a quick compatibility read without scanning the records.

bundle_digest.description: A BLAKE2b-256 fingerprint of the record SET: the record hashes sorted, each followed by a newline, hashed in order. Order-independent (hashes are sorted first) so the digest depends only on membership. A cheap whole-payload integrity pre-check on read — NOT the security boundary (per-record hash verification on ingest is).

blobs.description: Advisory totals for the `blobs/<sha256>` members, present only when the bundle carries blobs. The members themselves are the truth (self-verifying by name); this is a quick size read before extraction.

source.description: Optional advisory provenance: where these records were lifted from. Never a trust gate.

producer.description: DID of the signer, present when the manifest is signed (added by `--sign-seed`). Advisory provenance, not a trust gate.

signature.description: Optional Ed25519 signature over the canonical serialization of this manifest with the `signature` field removed. Since the manifest carries `bundle_digest`, the signature transitively attests to the record set. Advisory provenance only — a node still re-verifies every record by hash on ingest.
